summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates installer identity, git, SSH, and cloud metadata to hardcoded endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@1.3.1 is a decoy package impersonating the Anthropic SDK ecosystem (name 'anthropic-toolkit', description 'TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK', peerDependency on @anthropic-ai/sdk, repo pointing at a non-Anthropic org). The declared main entry 'dist/index.js' is not shipped in the tarball — the package has no real library surface. Its only effect is scripts/postinstall.js, which runs unconditionally on `npm install` and harvests installer-side identity and cloud metadata: os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), os.platform(), cwd, CI provider, project package.json name/author/repo, ~/.gitconfig user.email,.git/logs/HEAD committer emails, ~/.ssh/*.pub key comments, ~/.aws/config profile names, ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP project/account), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (gh CLI user/email), and /etc/resolv.conf search domain. The collected JSON is POSTed via https.request to hostname 'npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app'. The behavior is framed in comments as 'anonymous telemetry', but SSH public-key comments, git committer emails, and cloud account/project identifiers are still shipped off-host to a hardcoded author-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
